title: TC_FMD_LocationDenied_RepeatedPopupInSettings
steps: 1. Launch the app and begin onboarding flow.
2. When the location permission dialog appears during onboarding, tap 'Deny' (or equivalent negative action).
3. Complete the remaining onboarding steps.
4. Navigate to the FMD (Find My Device) main settings screen.
5. Observe whether a location permission dialog appears.
6. If a dialog appears, tap 'Deny' or 'Cancel' to dismiss it.
7. Wait 3-5 seconds and observe if the same location permission dialog reappears automatically.
8. Repeat steps 6-7 up to 5 times or until the dialog stops reappearing.
9. Note the number of times the dialog reappears.
expected: The location permission dialog should appear at most once when entering FMD settings. It should NOT repeatedly pop up (spam) after being dismissed. If the dialog reappears more than once without any user action triggering it, that is a bug.

title: TC_FMD_LocationDenied_EnableInSettings_AlertPlayButton
steps: 1. Launch the app and begin onboarding flow.
2. When the location permission dialog appears during onboarding, tap 'Deny'.
3. Complete the remaining onboarding steps.
4. Navigate to the FMD main settings screen.
5. If a location permission dialog appears, dismiss it.
6. Open Android system settings (via app info or system settings menu).
7. Navigate to App Permissions > Location.
8. Change the location permission from 'Denied' to 'Allow all the time' or 'Allow only while using the app'.
9. Return to the app's FMD main settings screen.
10. Locate the alert play button (or test alert/sound button) in FMD settings.
11. Tap the alert play button.
expected: After enabling location permission in Android settings, the alert play button in FMD settings should function correctly. Tapping it should trigger the alert sound/notification without any error or additional permission prompts. The app should recognize the newly granted location permission.

title: TC_FMD_LocationDenied_PermissionLoopInSettings_SpamCheck
steps: 1. Launch the app and begin onboarding flow.
2. When the location permission dialog appears during onboarding, tap 'Deny'.
3. Complete the remaining onboarding steps.
4. Navigate to the FMD main settings screen.
5. Observe any location permission dialog that appears. Dismiss it by tapping 'Deny' or 'Cancel'.
6. Wait 5 seconds and check if the dialog reappears automatically.
7. If the dialog reappears, dismiss it again and start a timer.
8. For the next 60 seconds, monitor the screen for any automatic reappearance of the location permission dialog.
9. Record each instance where the dialog appears without user interaction (excluding the initial appearance upon entering the screen).
10. After 60 seconds, if the dialog has appeared 3 or more times total (including the initial one), consider this a permission spam loop.
expected: The location permission dialog should NOT automatically reappear after being dismissed. The dialog should appear at most once upon entering the FMD settings screen. Any automatic re-prompting (spam) of the location permission dialog after dismissal constitutes a bug. The app should respect the user's denial and not repeatedly request the same permission.

title: TC_FMD_LocationDenied_OnboardingThenSettings_NavigationFlow
steps: 1. Launch the app fresh (clear data if needed).
2. Start the onboarding process.
3. When the location permission prompt appears, select 'Deny' or 'Don't allow'.
4. Complete all onboarding steps and reach the main app screen.
5. Navigate to Settings > FMD (Find My Device) settings.
6. Observe the screen for any permission-related dialogs.
7. Take note of the UI state: whether location-dependent features show as disabled, greyed out, or with appropriate messaging.
8. Check if there is any mechanism (toggle, button, link) to re-enable or request location again.
9. If a re-request mechanism exists, tap it and observe the permission dialog behavior.
10. Dismiss any dialogs and verify the app does not crash or enter an inconsistent state.
expected: After denying location during onboarding, the FMD settings screen should gracefully handle the denied state. Location-dependent features should be clearly indicated as unavailable (e.g., greyed out with a message). The app should not crash, freeze, or enter an inconsistent UI state. If a re-request mechanism exists, it should work correctly without causing a permission loop.

title: TC_FMD_LocationDenied_BackgroundLocation_RepeatedPrompt
steps: 1. Launch the app and begin onboarding flow.
2. When the location permission dialog appears during onboarding, tap 'Deny'.
3. Complete the remaining onboarding steps.
4. Navigate to the FMD main settings screen.
5. If a location permission dialog appears, dismiss it by tapping 'Deny'.
6. Put the app in the background (press Home button).
7. Wait 10 seconds, then bring the app back to the foreground.
8. Observe if the location permission dialog appears automatically.
9. If it appears, dismiss it and repeat steps 6-8 two more times.
10. Record whether the dialog appears upon each foregrounding event.
expected: The location permission dialog should NOT automatically appear when the app is brought back to the foreground after being backgrounded. The permission state should persist, and the app should not repeatedly prompt for location permission on every resume from background.
